label: Squarish 2BR + 1 T&B — public/private split, common bath between bedrooms with a hall notch

adjacencies.note: master's door opens onto the hall notch
standard's door opens onto the hall notch
master's south wall is common's north — solid, no door (bath access goes via the hall)
standard's north wall is common's south — solid, no door
common bath's east is the hall's west — bath door is on the hall side
single hallway-mouth bridge across the dividing wall — guests reach the bath via dining → hall → bath

notes: Compact 2BR + 1 T&B with a public/private zone split. The envelope is
partitioned by a vertical wall: LDK on one half, sleeping + bath + hall
Private column (front to rear): standard, [common T&B | hall notch],
master. The bath sits between the bedrooms; the hall is a small east-side
notch (against the dividing wall) that connects both bedrooms and the
bath, and bridges to dining via a single doorway.
Public column: living at front, dining + kitchen side-by-side at the rear.
No LDK front-to-rear stacking constraint — open-plan, casual feel.
Replaces the earlier non-split hallway variant, which had no real wall
between hall and LDK and was therefore functionally equivalent to the
off-dining single_bath topology. The wall here makes the hall do real
work: it buffers the bedrooms from LDK noise, and routes both bath access
and public bath access through a single controlled doorway.
